art_a: The sentence contains at least one digit.
art_b: The sentence is a question (ends with '?').
TT: Can you meet me at 3 o'clock?
Did you buy 2 loaves of bread?
How much is 5 dollars in euros?
Were there 7 people at the party?
Is the bus arriving in 10 minutes?
Have you read those 4 chapters yet?
Did the movie start at 8 tonight?
How many of the 12 cookies are left?
Is your flight at 7 in the morning?
Can we wait just 2 more minutes?
FF: I woke up early this morning and made a cup of coffee.
My sister called to say she would visit us next weekend.
The weather has been cold and rainy for the past few days.
We bought fresh vegetables and bread from the local market.
He forgot his umbrella at the office again yesterday.
The children played in the garden until it got dark.
I need to finish reading this book before the meeting tomorrow.
She planted some flowers near the front porch last spring.
Our neighbors invited us over for dinner on Friday evening.
The train was delayed by about twenty minutes this afternoon.
I always enjoy a quiet walk along the river after work.
My father taught me how to fix a flat tire when I was young.
We watched an old movie together and ate popcorn last night.
The bakery on the corner sells the best chocolate cake in town.
TF: I woke up at 6 this morning feeling refreshed.
We have 3 cats and 2 dogs at home.
The store closes at 9 on weekdays.
I drank 2 cups of coffee before work.
FT: Are you coming to the party tonight?
Did you remember to lock the door?
Can you pass me the salt, please?
What time should we leave tomorrow morning?
